Atomic-write idiom: serialize to STATE_FILE.with_name(name+'.tmp') in same directory, then tmp.replace(STATE_FILE). Path.replace() = atomic rename on same filesystem (os.replace/MoveFileEx); handles dest-exists on Windows. Crash before replace → STATE_FILE untouched (old state preserved); leftover .tmp harmless (next write overwrites).

V1 5 repeated writes → valid JSON + dest-exists replace OK; V2 no .tmp leftover; V3 tmp same-dir as STATE_FILE (atomic-rename precondition); V4 non-serializable state → json.dumps raises → STATE_FILE preserved unchanged (crash-safety property confirmed)

findings: F1 (BLOCK): relay XADD drops outbox row id; corrections.origin_event_id idempotency key is NOT on the wire — consumer can only see aggregate_id, so the UNIQUE(origin_service,origin_event_id) dedup is unenforceable as specified

F2 (BLOCK): aggregate_id is NOT a stable per-event id for KS/glossary — relay XADDs aggregate_id (entity/relation/event node id), which repeats across every correction to the same target; using it as origin_event_id collapses all corrections of one entity to a single row (lost corrections)

F3 (WARN): predicate_fix invalidate-then-recreate derives relation_id from (user,subj,predicate,obj); changing only the predicate yields a NEW relation_id so before!=after id is fine, BUT changing subject/object endpoints while the OLD predicate stays creates a NEW edge whose id equals an already-existing edge's id — create_relation MERGE silently reuses/promotes that pre-existing edge instead of making a distinct 'after', corrupting the before/after capture and possibly the live graph

BLOCK F4: outbox_id N-place sync is incomplete + miscited — EventData lives in dispatcher.py:19-28 not consumer.py; the relay XADD field name 'outbox_id' is never pinned to match _parse_event's fields.get() key, and the cloned learning-service consumer + DLQ writer are not enumerated as sync points

BLOCK F5: F3 resurrect-on-match fix corrupts the extraction pass2_writer caller — adding a valid_until=NULL clear to create_relation's ON MATCH branch (or a shared flag) silently resurrects user-invalidated edges on the next Pass-2 re-extraction of the same SVO; the only safe scoping is a dedicated recreate_relation, which the design lists only as an 'or' alternative

WARN F6: diff_class derivation table (§3) is order-dependent but order is unspecified for a rename-that-also-changes-kind and for op==merge with after=null; spurious-drop/missing-add rows are evaluated before kind-change/merge, so a rekind+rename lands as kind-change (loses boundary signal) and a destructive merge could mis-class as spurious-drop

findings.title: outbox_id default-empty-string silently re-introduces F2 when relay/consumer roll out of step
findings.detail: Spec 4.0 line 170 states a missing outbox_id defaults origin_event_id to '' and the ON CONFLICT (origin_service, origin_event_id) collapses every correction to ONE row per origin_service (the exact F2 bug this design exists to prevent). The chosen fix at line 172 is outbox_id: str = '' on EventData — a SILENT default. The 5-place sync is sequenced across BUILD sub-sessions (relay = Go session A; consumer parse = Python). If the relay XADD (point 1) is absent or rolled back independently of the Python parser default, every glossary/knowledge correction silently collapses to one row with NO error surfaced. The design acknowledges the hazard in prose but does not gate it. Recommend: learning-service reader treats empty/missing outbox_id as a hard error (DLQ the message) rather than INSERT with ''; or skip the ON CONFLICT INSERT entirely when outbox_id=='' and log ERROR. Default-'' on the dataclass is fine for ctor back-compat (all 4 construction sites use kwargs, verified), but the READER (point 5) must not silently accept it.

findings.title: recreate_relation resurrect: ON MATCH must also clear pending_validation/confidence-downgrade and capture-after ordering
findings.detail: _CREATE_RELATION_CYPHER ON MATCH (relations.py:183-197) does not set valid_until, so the dedicated recreate_relation correctly must clear it (F3/F5 mitigation feasible). But two adjacent edge-property hazards the spec does not pin: (a) when resurrecting a previously-invalidated tuple whose stored confidence>1.0-input, the create_relation ON MATCH confidence CASE keeps the OLD (higher) confidence and does NOT adopt the new pending_validation=false — a manual correction with confidence=1.0 will adopt new flags (1.0 beats any stored), so this specific path is safe, but recreate_relation is a NEW query (not create_relation) per F5, so it must replicate the max-confidence semantics deliberately or it diverges silently. (b) source_event_ids: a resurrected manual edge inherits the old extraction source_event_ids list; the captured after via get_relation will show stale provenance. Spec 6.4 says after is read post-write (good) but does not say recreate_relation resets source_event_ids for a manual authorship. Minor; flag for BUILD.

findings.title: diff_class reorder coherent but row-2 relation-update with predicate unchanged falls to row-7 'other' not 'boundary'
findings.detail: Walked the reordered table (3 op=invalidate->row4 spurious-drop correct; merge->row1 correct; entity rename+rekind->row5 kind-change correct; entity rename-only->row6 boundary correct). No WRONG-row regression found from the reorder — the probe hypothesis is clean. One coherence gap: a knowledge.relation_corrected op=update where predicate did NOT change (e.g. confidence/source edit) hits row2-no, row3-no(before present), row4-no(after present), row5-no(entity-gated), row6-no(entity-gated), row7 'other'. That is arguably correct (relation non-predicate edits are 'other'), but the table comment on row6 says 'entity & only name changed' — relation updates can never reach boundary. Acceptable by design; noting so BUILD does not 'fix' it into a relation-boundary branch. No block.

findings.what: Retry mechanism is dead in steady state. On handler failure _handle_failure increments retry_key and returns to 'leave pending for redelivery', but the run() loop only reads new messages with '>' and never reclaims pending (no XAUTOCLAIM/XCLAIM; _process_pending runs once at startup). A transiently-failing correction increments retry to 1, stays PEL forever, is never retried and never reaches MAX_RETRIES/DLQ until the next process restart. Drops corrections silently. Matches captured Redis-Streams delivery lesson.
findings.fix: Add a periodic pending-reclaim (XAUTOCLAIM with min-idle) inside the run() loop, or re-XADD on failure, so PEL messages get re-delivered and the retry counter advances.

findings.what: short_description is classified as CONTENT for entities, so a PATCH that edits ONLY short_description produces before/after content-hash mismatch and is labelled diff_class='boundary'. 'boundary' is meant for name/alias entity-boundary changes (merge/rename signal); a description tweak is not a boundary correction. Over-counts boundary in the stats that feed the eval-gold/few-shot tiers.
findings.fix: Either move short_description to its own content bucket separate from name/aliases, or split the entity content hash so 'boundary' fires only on name/aliases delta; classify description-only edits as 'other'.

findings.what: Making PATCH transactional changed its error semantics: an outbox INSERT failure (emitEntityUpdatedTx err) now rolls back the whole tx and returns 500, so a user edit the DB could have accepted fails because of a best-effort side-effect. createEntity already had this trade-off, but PATCH previously emitted best-effort; this is a regression in availability of the primary write. The design's own header comment (outbox.go:24-32) states emitting an event MUST NOT break the primary entity write.
findings.fix: If atomic before/after capture is required, keep the UPDATE+snapshot in-tx but downgrade the outbox-emit failure to best-effort-after-commit, OR consciously accept the stricter semantics and update the outbox.go contract comment to say PATCH is now strict-transactional.
